<command-name>/run-golden-tests</command-name>

content.text:
I'll help you run golden tests with complete schema validation, execution, and results analysis. This command provides a turnkey solution for executing tests in any golden tests repository.

**Usage**: `/run-golden-tests [test_pattern]`

**Examples**: 
- `/run-golden-tests` (run all tests)
- `/run-golden-tests core` (run tests matching "core" pattern)
- `/run-golden-tests hrf_*` (run tests matching specific pattern)

## Prerequisites Check and Setup

```bash
echo "Starting Golden Tests Execution with Schema Prevention..."

# Process command line argument (optional test pattern)
TEST_PATTERN_ARG="$1"

# Check if this is a golden tests directory
if [ -d "golden_tests" ]; then
    cd golden_tests
    echo "✓ Found golden_tests directory"
elif [ -f "validate_golden_tests.py" ] || [ -d "specs" ]; then
    echo "✓ Already in golden tests directory"
else
    echo "⚠️  No golden_tests directory found"
    echo "This command works from:"
    echo "  1. Root of repository with golden_tests/ subdirectory"
    echo "  2. Inside the golden_tests/ directory itself"
    echo ""
    read -p "Continue anyway? (y/n): " CONTINUE
    if [ "$CONTINUE" != "y" ]; then
        echo "Exiting. Run from correct directory or use 'init-golden-tests' first."
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Detect project language
echo "Detecting project language..."
if [ -f ../pyproject.toml ] || [ -f ../setup.py ] || find .. -name "*.py" -type f | head -1 | grep -q .; then
    PROJECT_LANG="Python"
    echo "✓ Detected Python project"
elif [ -f ../Cargo.toml ] || find .. -name "*.rs" -type f | head -1 | grep -q .; then
    PROJECT_LANG="Rust"
    echo "✓ Detected Rust project"
elif [ -f ../DESCRIPTION ] || find .. -name "*.R" -type f | head -1 | grep -q .; then
    PROJECT_LANG="R"
    echo "✓ Detected R project"
elif [ -f ../Project.toml ] || find .. -name "*.jl" -type f | head -1 | grep -q .; then
    PROJECT_LANG="Julia"
    echo "✓ Detected Julia project"
else
    PROJECT_LANG="Unknown"
    echo "⚠️  Could not detect project language - will prompt for manual selection"
fi

# Set test pattern
if [ -n "$TEST_PATTERN_ARG" ]; then
    TEST_PATTERN="$TEST_PATTERN_ARG"
    echo "Using test pattern: $TEST_PATTERN"
else
    TEST_PATTERN=""
    echo "Running all tests"
fi
```

## Step 1: Critical Schema Validation

```bash
echo ""
echo "🔒 STEP 1: SCHEMA VALIDATION (CRITICAL)"
echo "================================================"

# Check for schema validator
if [ ! -f validate_golden_tests.py ]; then
    echo "❌ Schema validator not found - attempting installation..."
    
    # Try to find validator from known locations
    VALIDATOR_SOURCE=""
    if [ -f ~/code/fmrihrf/golden_tests/tools/validate_golden_tests.py ]; then
        VALIDATOR_SOURCE=~/code/fmrihrf/golden_tests/tools/validate_golden_tests.py
    elif [ -f ~/code/translation/golden/tools/validate_golden_tests.py ]; then
        VALIDATOR_SOURCE=~/code/translation/golden/tools/validate_golden_tests.py
    fi
    
    if [ -n "$VALIDATOR_SOURCE" ]; then
        cp "$VALIDATOR_SOURCE" .
        chmod +x validate_golden_tests.py
        echo "✓ Schema validator installed"
    else
        echo "❌ Schema validator not found - tests may fail due to schema issues"
        echo "Install manually: cp /path/to/reference/repo/tools/validate_golden_tests.py ."
        echo ""
        read -p "Continue without schema validation? (y/n): " CONTINUE_NO_VALIDATOR
        if [ "$CONTINUE_NO_VALIDATOR" != "y" ]; then
            echo "Exiting. Install schema validator first."
            exit 1
        fi
    fi
fi

# Run schema validation
if [ -f validate_golden_tests.py ]; then
    echo "Running comprehensive schema validation..."
    python validate_golden_tests.py --project . --verbose
    SCHEMA_RESULT=$?
    
    if [ $SCHEMA_RESULT -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "✅ All XML files pass canonical schema validation"
        echo "🔒 Schema Prevention: All tests use xmlns=\"http://golden-tests.org/schema\""
    else
        echo "❌ Schema validation failed - tests may not be discovered properly"
        echo ""
        echo "Common schema fixes:"
        echo "  1. Use canonical namespace: xmlns=\"http://golden-tests.org/schema\""
        echo "  2. Remove deprecated xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation attributes"
        echo "  3. Validate XML structure and required elements"
        echo ""
        read -p "Continue with schema violations? (y/n): " CONTINUE_WITH_VIOLATIONS
        if [ "$CONTINUE_WITH_VIOLATIONS" != "y" ]; then
            echo "Exiting. Fix schema issues first."
            exit 1
        fi
    fi
else
    echo "⚠️  Skipping schema validation (validator not available)"
fi

echo ""
```

## Step 2: Test Discovery and Validation

```bash
echo "🔍 STEP 2: TEST DISCOVERY"
echo "================================================"

# Count total test specifications
TOTAL_SPECS=$(find specs -name "*.xml" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$TOTAL_SPECS" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "❌ No test specifications found in specs/ directory"
    echo "Ensure you're in the correct directory or run 'sync-golden-tests' first"
    exit 1
fi

echo "📋 Total test specifications found: $TOTAL_SPECS"

# Apply test pattern filtering if specified
if [ -n "$TEST_PATTERN" ]; then
    FILTERED_SPECS=$(find specs -name "*.xml" | grep "$TEST_PATTERN" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
    echo "🔍 Tests matching pattern '$TEST_PATTERN': $FILTERED_SPECS"
    if [ "$FILTERED_SPECS" -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "❌ No tests match the specified pattern"
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Verify test runner availability
RUNNER_MISSING=false
case $PROJECT_LANG in
    "Python")
        if [ ! -f golden_test_runner.py ] && [ ! -f improved_test_runner.py ]; then
            echo "⚠️  Python test runner not found"
            RUNNER_MISSING=true
        else
            echo "✓ Python test runner available"
        fi
        ;;
    "R")
        if [ ! -f golden_test_runner.R ]; then
            echo "⚠️  R test runner not found"
            RUNNER_MISSING=true
        else
            echo "✓ R test runner available"
        fi
        ;;
    *)
        echo "⚠️  Generic test execution - check for language-specific runners"
        ;;
esac

if [ "$RUNNER_MISSING" = "true" ]; then
    echo "Installing missing test runners..."
    # Try to copy from known locations
    if [ -d ~/code/fmrihrf/golden_tests/tools ]; then
        cp ~/code/fmrihrf/golden_tests/tools/golden_test_runner.* . 2>/dev/null
        cp ~/code/fmrihrf/golden_tests/tools/improved_test_runner.py . 2>/dev/null
        chmod +x *.py *.R 2>/dev/null
        echo "✓ Test runners installed"
    else
        echo "❌ Could not install test runners automatically"
        echo "Copy manually from reference repository tools/ directory"
    fi
fi

echo ""
```

## Step 3: Execute Tests by Language

```bash
echo "🧪 STEP 3: TEST EXECUTION"
echo "================================================"

# Generate timestamp for results
TIMESTAMP=$(date +"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
RESULTS_DIR="results_$TIMESTAMP"
mkdir -p "$RESULTS_DIR"

# Execute tests based on detected language
EXECUTION_SUCCESS=false

case $PROJECT_LANG in
    "Python")
        echo "Running Python tests..."
        PYTHON_RESULTS="golden_test_results_python_$TIMESTAMP.json"
        
        if [ -f improved_test_runner.py ]; then
            PYTHON_CMD="python improved_test_runner.py --golden-tests-dir . --output $PYTHON_RESULTS --verbose"
        elif [ -f golden_test_runner.py ]; then
            PYTHON_CMD="python golden_test_runner.py --output $PYTHON_RESULTS --verbose"
        else
            echo "❌ No Python test runner available"
            PYTHON_CMD=""
        fi
        
        if [ -n "$PYTHON_CMD" ]; then
            # Add test pattern if specified
            if [ -n "$TEST_PATTERN" ]; then
                PYTHON_CMD="$PYTHON_CMD --pattern $TEST_PATTERN"
            fi
            
            echo "Executing: $PYTHON_CMD"
            eval $PYTHON_CMD
            PYTHON_EXIT_CODE=$?
            
            if [ $PYTHON_EXIT_CODE -eq 0 ] && [ -f "$PYTHON_RESULTS" ]; then
                echo "✅ Python tests completed successfully"
                mv "$PYTHON_RESULTS" "$RESULTS_DIR/"
                EXECUTION_SUCCESS=true
                
                # Show summary
                PYTHON_SUMMARY=$(python -c "
import json
with open('$RESULTS_DIR/$PYTHON_RESULTS') as f: 
    data = json.load(f)
    s = data['summary']
    print(f'Python Results: {s[\"passed_tests\"]}/{s[\"total_tests\"]} passed, {s[\"failed_tests\"]} failed, {s[\"error_tests\"]} errors')
" 2>/dev/null)
                echo "📊 $PYTHON_SUMMARY"
            else
                echo "❌ Python tests failed or no results generated"
            fi
        fi
        ;;
        
    "R")
        echo "Running R tests..."
        R_RESULTS="golden_test_results_r_$TIMESTAMP.json"
        
        if [ -f golden_test_runner.R ]; then
            R_CMD="Rscript golden_test_runner.R --output $R_RESULTS --verbose"
            
            # Add test pattern if specified
            if [ -n "$TEST_PATTERN" ]; then
                R_CMD="$R_CMD --pattern $TEST_PATTERN"
            fi
            
            echo "Executing: $R_CMD"
            eval $R_CMD
            R_EXIT_CODE=$?
            
            if [ $R_EXIT_CODE -eq 0 ] && [ -f "$R_RESULTS" ]; then
                echo "✅ R tests completed successfully"
                mv "$R_RESULTS" "$RESULTS_DIR/"
                EXECUTION_SUCCESS=true
                
                # Show summary
                R_SUMMARY=$(Rscript -e "
library(jsonlite)
data <- fromJSON('$RESULTS_DIR/$R_RESULTS')
s <- data\$summary
cat(sprintf('R Results: %d/%d passed, %d failed, %d errors\\n', 
    s\$passed_tests, s\$total_tests, s\$failed_tests, s\$error_tests))
" 2>/dev/null)
                echo "📊 $R_SUMMARY"
            else
                echo "❌ R tests failed or no results generated"
            fi
        else
            echo "❌ R test runner not available"
        fi
        ;;
        
    *)
        echo "⚠️  Unknown language - attempting generic test execution"
        if [ -f golden_test_runner.py ]; then
            echo "Trying Python runner..."
            python golden_test_runner.py --verbose
            [ $? -eq 0 ] && EXECUTION_SUCCESS=true
        elif [ -f golden_test_runner.R ]; then
            echo "Trying R runner..."
            Rscript golden_test_runner.R --verbose
            [ $? -eq 0 ] && EXECUTION_SUCCESS=true
        else
            echo "❌ No test runners available"
        fi
        ;;
esac

if [ "$EXECUTION_SUCCESS" = "false" ]; then
    echo "❌ Test execution failed - check test runners and implementations"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
```

## Step 4: Results Analysis and Dashboard Generation

```bash
echo "📊 STEP 4: RESULTS ANALYSIS AND DASHBOARD"
echo "================================================"

# Find latest results files
LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS=$(ls "$RESULTS_DIR"/golden_test_results_python_*.json 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
LATEST_R_RESULTS=$(ls "$RESULTS_DIR"/golden_test_results_r_*.json 2>/dev/null | tail -1)

if [ -z "$LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS" ] && [ -z "$LATEST_R_RESULTS" ]; then
    echo "⚠️  No results files found - dashboard generation skipped"
else
    echo "Generating interactive dashboard..."
    
    # Check for dashboard generator
    if [ ! -f enhanced_dashboard_generator.py ]; then
        echo "Installing dashboard generator..."
        if [ -f ~/code/fmrihrf/golden_tests/tools/enhanced_dashboard_generator.py ]; then
            cp ~/code/fmrihrf/golden_tests/tools/enhanced_dashboard_generator.py .
            chmod +x enhanced_dashboard_generator.py
            echo "✓ Dashboard generator installed"
        else
            echo "⚠️  Dashboard generator not found - manual installation required"
        fi
    fi
    
    if [ -f enhanced_dashboard_generator.py ]; then
        DASHBOARD_DIR="dashboard_$TIMESTAMP"
        
        # Generate dashboard based on available results
        if [ -n "$LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS" ] && [ -n "$LATEST_R_RESULTS" ]; then
            # Multi-language dashboard
            if [ -f comparison_dashboard_generator.py ]; then
                python comparison_dashboard_generator.py \
                    --project . \
                    --python-results "$LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS" \
                    --r-results "$LATEST_R_RESULTS" \
                    --output "$DASHBOARD_DIR"
                echo "✅ Multi-language comparison dashboard generated"
            else
                # Single dashboard with latest results
                python enhanced_dashboard_generator.py \
                    --project . \
                    --results "$LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS" \
                    --output "$DASHBOARD_DIR"
                echo "✅ Dashboard generated from Python results"
            fi
        elif [ -n "$LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS" ]; then
            python enhanced_dashboard_generator.py \
                --project . \
                --results "$LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS" \
                --output "$DASHBOARD_DIR"
            echo "✅ Dashboard generated from Python results"
        elif [ -n "$LATEST_R_RESULTS" ]; then
            python enhanced_dashboard_generator.py \
                --project . \
                --results "$LATEST_R_RESULTS" \
                --output "$DASHBOARD_DIR"
            echo "✅ Dashboard generated from R results"
        fi
        
        if [ -f "$DASHBOARD_DIR/index.html" ]; then
            echo "📊 Dashboard available at: $DASHBOARD_DIR/index.html"
        fi
    fi
fi

echo ""
```

## Step 5: Comprehensive Summary Report

```bash
echo "================================================"
echo "🎯 GOLDEN TESTS EXECUTION SUMMARY"
echo "================================================"

echo "📁 Execution Details:"
echo "   Project Language: $PROJECT_LANG"
echo "   Test Pattern: ${TEST_PATTERN:-'All tests'}"
echo "   Execution Time: $(date)"
echo "   Results Directory: $RESULTS_DIR"
echo ""

# Schema Compliance Report
echo "🔒 Schema Compliance:"
if [ -f validate_golden_tests.py ]; then
    SCHEMA_STATS=$(python validate_golden_tests.py --project . 2>/dev/null | grep "Total files\|Valid files" | tr '\n' ' ')
    if [ -n "$SCHEMA_STATS" ]; then
        echo "   $SCHEMA_STATS"
    else
        echo "   ✅ Schema validation completed successfully"
    fi
else
    echo "   ⚠️  Schema validation not performed"
fi
echo ""

# Test Results Summary
echo "🧪 Test Results:"
if [ -n "$LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS" ]; then
    PYTHON_DETAILED=$(python -c "
import json
with open('$LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS') as f: 
    data = json.load(f)
    s = data['summary']
    print(f'   🐍 Python: {s[\"passed_tests\"]}/{s[\"total_tests\"]} passed ({s[\"passed_tests\"]/s[\"total_tests\"]*100:.1f}%), {s[\"failed_tests\"]} failed, {s[\"error_tests\"]} errors')
" 2>/dev/null)
    echo "$PYTHON_DETAILED"
fi

if [ -n "$LATEST_R_RESULTS" ]; then
    R_DETAILED=$(Rscript -e "
library(jsonlite)
data <- fromJSON('$LATEST_R_RESULTS')
s <- data\$summary
cat(sprintf('   📊 R: %d/%d passed (%.1f%%), %d failed, %d errors\\n', 
    s\$passed_tests, s\$total_tests, s\$passed_tests/s\$total_tests*100, 
    s\$failed_tests, s\$error_tests))
" 2>/dev/null)
    echo "$R_DETAILED"
fi

if [ -z "$LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS" ] && [ -z "$LATEST_R_RESULTS" ]; then
    echo "   ⚠️  No detailed results available"
fi
echo ""

# Dashboard Information
echo "📊 Generated Artifacts:"
if [ -f "$DASHBOARD_DIR/index.html" ]; then
    echo "   📈 Interactive Dashboard: $DASHBOARD_DIR/index.html"
fi
echo "   📁 Results Directory: $RESULTS_DIR/"
if [ -n "$LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS" ]; then
    echo "   🐍 Python Results: $LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS"
fi
if [ -n "$LATEST_R_RESULTS" ]; then
    echo "   📊 R Results: $LATEST_R_RESULTS"
fi
echo ""

# Quick Actions for Next Steps
echo "⚡ Quick Actions:"
echo ""
echo "🔄 Re-run Tests:"
echo "   /run-golden-tests                    # All tests"
echo "   /run-golden-tests $TEST_PATTERN      # Same pattern"
echo ""
echo "🔍 View Results:"
if [ -f "$DASHBOARD_DIR/index.html" ]; then
    echo "   open $DASHBOARD_DIR/index.html      # Open dashboard"
fi
echo "   cat $LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS | jq .   # View JSON results"
echo ""
echo "🔧 Debug Issues:"
echo "   python validate_golden_tests.py --project . --verbose  # Check schema"
echo "   python golden_test_runner.py --verbose --debug         # Debug mode"
echo ""
echo "📊 Generate Fresh Dashboard:"
if [ -n "$LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS" ]; then
    echo "   python enhanced_dashboard_generator.py --project . --results $LATEST_PYTHON_RESULTS"
fi

echo ""
echo "================================================"
echo "✅ Golden tests execution complete!"
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "1. Review results in dashboard or JSON files"
echo "2. Fix any failing tests by updating implementations"
echo "3. Re-run tests to validate fixes"
echo "4. Check GOLDEN_TESTS.md for methodology details"
echo "================================================"
```

This comprehensive run command provides:

1. **🔒 Schema validation** as the critical first step
2. **🔍 Test discovery** with pattern filtering support
3. **🧪 Multi-language execution** (Python, R, Rust, Julia)
4. **📊 Dashboard generation** with interactive results
5. **📁 Organized results** with timestamped directories
6. **⚡ Quick actions** for follow-up workflows
7. **🔧 Troubleshooting** guidance and debugging tools

The command is completely turnkey and ensures schema compliance before test execution to prevent the discovery issues that were resolved in previous work!
I'll help you run the golden tests. Let me first check what's in the golden_tests directory and then execute the tests.

### 🧪 Test Results

**📊 R Tests**: 10/11 passed (90.9%), 1 failed, 0 errors  
**🐍 Python Tests**: 2/11 passed (18.2%), 0 failed, 9 errors

### 📈 Key Findings

- **R Implementation**: Nearly complete with only 1 failing test in `lag_hrf_decorator`
- **Python Implementation**: Missing core R functions (`evaluate`, `attr`, `nbasis`, etc.)
- **Schema Compliance**: Perfect - all tests properly formatted

### 🔍 Failed Test Details

1. **Fix R test**: Debug the `lag_hrf_decorator` calculation issue
2. **Python implementation**: Add missing R function equivalents
3. **Re-run tests**: Validate fixes with `/run-golden-tests`

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Improved Golden Test Runner for Python Implementations

Handles R-style expressions in test specifications and properly executes
Python implementations with better error handling and R-to-Python translation.
"""

import os
import sys
import json
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import numpy as np
import traceback
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Any, Optional, Tuple, Union
from dataclasses import dataclass, asdict
from datetime import datetime
import argparse
import textwrap

1→<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
     2→<golden_test xmlns="http://golden-tests.org/schema">
     3→  <metadata>
     4→    <id>lag_hrf_decorator</id>
     5→    <version>1.0</version>
     6→    <description>Temporal lag decorator for HRF functions</description>
     7→    <tags>
     8→      <tag>core</tag>
     9→      <tag>hrf</tag>
    10→      <tag>decorator</tag>
    11→      <tag>lag</tag>
    12→      <tag>time_shift</tag>
    13→    </tags>
    14→  </metadata>
    15→  
    16→  <semantic_description>
    17→    <purpose>Validate the lag_hrf function that applies temporal lag to existing HRF objects</purpose>
    18→    <algorithm>
    19→      The lag_hrf function is a decorator that applies a temporal lag to an existing HRF object.
    20→      This creates a time-shifted version of the hemodynamic response, which is useful for 
    21→      modeling delayed responses or temporal variations in neural processing.
    22→      
    23→      Mathematical behavior:
    24→      - Time shifting: f_lagged(t) = f_original(t - lag)
    25→      - Positive lag shifts response later in time (delayed response)
    26→      - Negative lag shifts response earlier in time (anticipated response)
    27→      - Preserves shape: only temporal position changes
    28→      - Span adjustment: span_new = span_original + max(0, lag)
    29→    </algorithm>
    30→    <mathematical_properties>
    31→      Key properties to validate:
    32→      1. Time shift accuracy: exact temporal displacement
    33→      2. Shape preservation: response magnitude unchanged at corresponding times
    34→      3. Span adjustment: proper domain extension for positive lags
    35→      4. Attribute inheritance: nbasis, name modification, parameter tracking
    36→      5. Edge case handling: zero lag, negative lag, large lag values
    37→      6. Mathematical equivalence: lag_hrf(h, L)(t) ≡ h(t - L)
    38→    </mathematical_properties>
    39→    <edge_cases>
    40→      - Zero lag: should produce identical output to original
    41→      - Negative lag: shifts response earlier, span unchanged
    42→      - Large lag: significant span increase, delayed response
    43→      - Multi-basis HRFs: preserve basis count and matrix structure
    44→    </edge_cases>
    45→  </semantic_description>
    46→  
    47→  <inputs>
    48→    <input name="eval_times" type="numeric_vector">
    49→      <description>Time points for evaluation</description>
    50→      <value>c(0.0, 2.0, 5.0, 8.0, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0)</value>
    51→    </input>
    52→    <input name="comparison_times" type="numeric_vector">
    53→      <description>Time points for equivalence checking</description>
    54→      <value>c(-3.0, -1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 7.0, 12.0, 17.0)</value>
    55→    </input>
    56→    <input name="search_times" type="numeric_vector">
    57→      <description>Time points for peak timing validation</description>
    58→      <value>c(8.0, 9.0, 10.0, 11.0, 12.0)</value>
    59→    </input>
    60→  </inputs>
    61→  
    62→  <expected_outputs>
    63→    <numeric_checks>
    64→      <!-- Positive lag shift validation -->
    65→      <check>
    66→        <type>approximate</type>
    67→        <location>evaluate(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0), eval_times)</location>
    68→        <expected>[0.0, 0.0, 0.3716, 0.1253, 0.0498, 0.0045, 0.0005]</expected>
    69→        <tolerance>1e-12</tolerance>
    70→      </check>
    71→      
    72→      <!-- Span adjustment for positive lag -->
    73→      <check>
    74→        <type>exact_value</type>
    75→        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0), "span")</location>

    78→      </check>
    79→

    86→      </check>
    87→      
    88→      <!-- Name modification -->
    89→      <check>

    94→      </check>
    95→

    97→      <check>
    98→        <type>exact_value</type>
    99→        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_gaussian_obj, -2.0), "span")</location>
   100→        <expected>30.0</expected>

   102→      </check>
   103→

   151→      hrf_gamma_obj <- as_hrf(function(t) hrf_gamma(t, shape = 6, rate = 1), name = "gamma", nbasis = 1, span = 30)
   152→      hrf_bspline_obj <- as_hrf(function(t) hrf_bspline(t, N = 4), name = "bspline", nbasis = 4, span = 24)

   165→      name_modification <- attr(lagged_spmg1, "name")

   169→      negative_lag_values <- evaluate(lagged_gaussian_neg, eval_times)

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<golden_test xmlns="http://golden-tests.org/schema">
  <metadata>
    <id>lag_hrf_decorator</id>
    <version>1.0</version>
    <description>Temporal lag decorator for HRF functions</description>
    <tags>
      <tag>core</tag>
      <tag>hrf</tag>
      <tag>decorator</tag>
      <tag>lag</tag>
      <tag>time_shift</tag>
    </tags>
  </metadata>
  
  <semantic_description>
    <purpose>Validate the lag_hrf function that applies temporal lag to existing HRF objects</purpose>
    <algorithm>
      The lag_hrf function is a decorator that applies a temporal lag to an existing HRF object.
      This creates a time-shifted version of the hemodynamic response, which is useful for 
      modeling delayed responses or temporal variations in neural processing.
      
      Mathematical behavior:
      - Time shifting: f_lagged(t) = f_original(t - lag)
      - Positive lag shifts response later in time (delayed response)
      - Negative lag shifts response earlier in time (anticipated response)
      - Preserves shape: only temporal position changes
      - Span adjustment: span_new = span_original + max(0, lag)
    </algorithm>
    <mathematical_properties>
      Key properties to validate:
      1. Time shift accuracy: exact temporal displacement
      2. Shape preservation: response magnitude unchanged at corresponding times
      3. Span adjustment: proper domain extension for positive lags
      4. Attribute inheritance: nbasis, name modification, parameter tracking
      5. Edge case handling: zero lag, negative lag, large lag values
      6. Mathematical equivalence: lag_hrf(h, L)(t) ≡ h(t - L)
    </mathematical_properties>
    <edge_cases>
      - Zero lag: should produce identical output to original
      - Negative lag: shifts response earlier, span unchanged
      - Large lag: significant span increase, delayed response
      - Multi-basis HRFs: preserve basis count and matrix structure
    </edge_cases>
  </semantic_description>
  
  <inputs>
    <input name="eval_times" type="numeric_vector">
      <description>Time points for evaluation</description>
      <value>c(0.0, 2.0, 5.0, 8.0, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0)</value>
    </input>
    <input name="comparison_times" type="numeric_vector">
      <description>Time points for equivalence checking</description>
      <value>c(-3.0, -1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 7.0, 12.0, 17.0)</value>
    </input>
    <input name="search_times" type="numeric_vector">
      <description>Time points for peak timing validation</description>
      <value>c(8.0, 9.0, 10.0, 11.0, 12.0)</value>
    </input>
  </inputs>
  
  <expected_outputs>
    <numeric_checks>
      <!-- Positive lag shift validation -->
      <check>
        <type>approximate</type>
        <location>evaluate(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0), eval_times)</location>
        <expected>[0.0, 0.0, 0.3716, 0.1253, 0.0498, 0.0045, 0.0005]</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-12</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Span adjustment for positive lag -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0), "span")</location>
        <expected>33.0</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-10</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Basis preservation -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>nbasis(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0))</location>
        <expected>1</expected>
        <tolerance>0</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Name modification -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0), "name")</location>
        <expected>spmg1_lag(3)</expected>
        <tolerance>0</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Negative lag: span unchanged -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_gaussian_obj, -2.0), "span")</location>
        <expected>30.0</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-10</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Zero lag identity -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_gamma_obj, 0.0), "span")</location>
        <expected>30.0</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-10</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Large lag boundary -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 25.0), "span")</location>
        <expected>55.0</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-10</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Peak timing shift validation -->
      <check>
        <type>approximate</type>
        <location>max(evaluate(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 4.0), search_times))</location>
        <expected>0.4556</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-12</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Multi-basis preservation -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>nbasis(lag_hrf(hrf_bspline_obj, 2.5))</location>
        <expected>4</expected>
        <tolerance>0</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Multi-basis span adjustment -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_bspline_obj, 2.5), "span")</location>
        <expected>26.5</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-10</tolerance>
      </check>
    </numeric_checks>
  </expected_outputs>
  
  <implementations>
    <R><![CDATA[
      # Create base HRFs for testing
      hrf_spmg1_obj <- as_hrf(hrf_spmg1, name = "spmg1", nbasis = 1, span = 30)
      hrf_gaussian_obj <- as_hrf(function(t) hrf_gaussian(t, mean = 6, sd = 2), name = "gaussian", nbasis = 1, span = 30)
      hrf_gamma_obj <- as_hrf(function(t) hrf_gamma(t, shape = 6, rate = 1), name = "gamma", nbasis = 1, span = 30)
      hrf_bspline_obj <- as_hrf(function(t) hrf_bspline(t, N = 4), name = "bspline", nbasis = 4, span = 24)
      
      # Input vectors
      eval_times <- c(0.0, 2.0, 5.0, 8.0, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0)
      comparison_times <- c(-3.0, -1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 7.0, 12.0, 17.0)
      search_times <- c(8.0, 9.0, 10.0, 11.0, 12.0)
      
      # Test positive lag shift
      lagged_spmg1 <- lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0)
      lagged_values <- evaluate(lagged_spmg1, eval_times)
      equivalence_check <- evaluate(hrf_spmg1_obj, comparison_times)
      span_adjustment <- attr(lagged_spmg1, "span")
      nbasis_preserved <- nbasis(lagged_spmg1)
      name_modification <- attr(lagged_spmg1, "name")
      
      # Test negative lag shift
      lagged_gaussian_neg <- lag_hrf(hrf_gaussian_obj, -2.0)
      negative_lag_values <- evaluate(lagged_gaussian_neg, eval_times)
      span_no_increase <- attr(lagged_gaussian_neg, "span")
      name_negative_lag <- attr(lagged_gaussian_neg, "name")
      
      # Test zero lag identity
      lagged_gamma_zero <- lag_hrf(hrf_gamma_obj, 0.0)
      identity_values <- evaluate(lagged_gamma_zero, eval_times)
      span_unchanged <- attr(lagged_gamma_zero, "span")
      name_zero_lag <- attr(lagged_gamma_zero, "name")
      
      # Test large lag boundary
      lagged_spmg1_large <- lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 25.0)
      large_lag_values <- evaluate(lagged_spmg1_large, eval_times)
      large_span_increase <- attr(lagged_spmg1_large, "span")
      
      # Test peak timing shift
      lagged_spmg1_peak <- lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 4.0)
      peak_values <- evaluate(lagged_spmg1_peak, search_times)
      peak_shifted_correctly <- which.max(peak_values) == which.max(evaluate(hrf_spmg1_obj, search_times - 4.0))
      peak_magnitude_preserved <- max(peak_values)
      
      # Test multi-basis preservation
      lagged_bspline <- lag_hrf(hrf_bspline_obj, 2.5)
      multi_basis_result <- evaluate(lagged_bspline, eval_times)
      multi_basis_shape <- dim(multi_basis_result)
      nbasis_multi_preserved <- nbasis(lagged_bspline)
      span_multi_adjusted <- attr(lagged_bspline, "span")
    ]]></R>
    
    <Python><![CDATA[
      # Python implementation using closure pattern
      # Create a function that returns a closure capturing the lag value.
      # Use functools.wraps or similar for metadata preservation.
      # Ensure span calculation handles max(0, lag) correctly.
      # Implementation pending - refer to R reference for mathematical behavior
    ]]></Python>
    
    <Rust><![CDATA[
      // Rust implementation using higher-order functions or traits
      // Consider struct wrapping original HRF with lag field, or 
      // function returning Box<dyn Fn> for dynamic dispatch.
      // Ensure proper ownership and lifetime management.
      // Implementation pending - refer to R reference for mathematical behavior
    ]]></Rust>
  </implementations>
  
  <propagation_status>
    <implementation lang="R" status="completed" date="2024-01-15"/>
    <implementation lang="Python" status="pending"/>
    <implementation lang="Rust" status="pending"/>
  </propagation_status>
</golden_test>

1→#' Lag an HRF Object
     2→#'
     3→#' Creates a new HRF object by applying a temporal lag to an existing HRF object.
     4→#'
     5→#' @param hrf The HRF object (of class `HRF`) to lag.
     6→#' @param lag The time lag in seconds to apply. Positive values shift the response later in time.
     7→#'
     8→#' @return A new HRF object representing the lagged function.
     9→#'
    10→#' @family HRF_decorator_functions
    11→#' @export
    12→#' @examples
    13→#' lagged_spmg1 <- lag_hrf(HRF_SPMG1, 5)
    14→#' # Evaluate at time 10; equivalent to HRF_SPMG1(10 - 5)
    15→#' lagged_spmg1(10)
    16→#' HRF_SPMG1(5)
    17→lag_hrf <- function(hrf, lag) {
    18→  assertthat::assert_that(inherits(hrf, "HRF"), msg = "Input 'hrf' must be an HRF object.")
    19→  assertthat::assert_that(
    20→    is.numeric(lag) && length(lag) == 1 && is.finite(lag),
    21→    msg = "'lag' must be a single finite numeric value."
    22→  )
    23→
    24→  # Original attributes
    25→  orig_name <- attr(hrf, "name")
    26→  orig_span <- attr(hrf, "span")
    27→  orig_nbasis <- nbasis(hrf)
    28→  orig_params <- attr(hrf, "params")
    29→
    30→  # Create the lagged function
    31→  lagged_func <- function(t) {
    32→    hrf(t - lag)
    33→  }
    34→
    35→  # Create new HRF object using as_hrf
    36→  as_hrf(
    37→    f = lagged_func,
    38→    name = paste0(orig_name, "_lag(", lag, ")"),
    39→    nbasis = orig_nbasis,
    40→    span = orig_span + max(0, lag), # Increase span if lag is positive
    41→    params = c(orig_params, list(.lag = lag)) # Add lag to params for bookkeeping
    42→  )
    43→}
    44→
    45→
    46→#' Create a Blocked HRF Object
    47→#'
    48→#' Creates a new HRF object representing a response to a sustained (blocked)
    49→#' stimulus by convolving the input HRF with a boxcar function of a given width.
    50→#'
    51→#' @param hrf The HRF object (of class `HRF`) to block.
    52→#' @param width The width of the block in seconds.
    53→#' @param precision The sampling precision in seconds used for the internal convolution (default: 0.1).
    54→#' @param half_life The half-life of an optional exponential decay applied during the block (default: Inf, meaning no decay).
    55→#' @param summate Logical; if TRUE (default), the responses from each time point within the block are summed. If FALSE, the maximum response at each time point is taken.
    56→#' @param normalize Logical; if TRUE, the resulting blocked HRF is scaled so that its peak value is 1 (default: FALSE).
    57→#'
    58→#' @return A new HRF object representing the blocked function.
    59→#'
    60→#' @family HRF_decorator_functions
    61→#' @export
    62→#' @examples
    63→#' blocked_spmg1 <- block_hrf(HRF_SPMG1, width = 5)
    64→#' t_vals <- seq(0, 30, by = 0.5)
    65→#' plot(t_vals, HRF_SPMG1(t_vals), type = 'l', col = "blue", ylab = "Response", xlab = "Time")
    66→#' lines(t_vals, blocked_spmg1(t_vals), col = "red")
    67→#' legend("topright", legend = c("Original", "Blocked (width=5)"), col = c("blue", "red"), lty = 1)
    68→block_hrf <- function(hrf, width, precision = 0.1, half_life = Inf, summate = TRUE, normalize = FALSE) {
    69→  assertthat::assert_that(inherits(hrf, "HRF"), msg = "Input 'hrf' must be an HRF object.")
    70→  assertthat::assert_that(
    71→    is.numeric(width) && length(width) == 1 && width >= 0 && is.finite(width),
    72→    msg = "'width' must be a single non-negative finite numeric value."
    73→  )
    74→  assertthat::assert_that(
    75→    is.numeric(precision) && length(precision) == 1 && precision > 0 && is.finite(precision),
    76→    msg = "'precision' must be a single finite positive numeric value."
    77→  )
    78→  assertthat::assert_that(
    79→    is.numeric(half_life) && length(half_life) == 1 && half_life > 0,
    80→    msg = "'half_life' must be a single positive numeric value."
    81→  )
    82→  assertthat::assert_that(is.logical(summate) && length(summate) == 1, msg = "'summate' must be a single logical value.")
    83→  assertthat::assert_that(is.logical(normalize) && length(normalize) == 1, msg = "'normalize' must be a single logical value.")
    84→
    85→  # Original attributes
    86→  orig_name <- attr(hrf, "name")
    87→  orig_span <- attr(hrf, "span")
    88→  orig_nbasis <- nbasis(hrf)
    89→  orig_params <- attr(hrf, "params")
    90→
    91→  # Create the blocked function
    92→  blocked_func <- function(t) {
    93→    if (width < precision) {
    94→      # If width is negligible, just return the original hrf value
    95→      res <- hrf(t)
    96→    } else {
    97→      samples <- seq(0, width, by = precision)
    98→      hmat_list <- lapply(samples, function(offset) {
    99→        decay_factor <- if (is.infinite(half_life)) 1 else exp(-log(2) * offset / half_life)
   100→        hrf(t - offset) * decay_factor
   101→      })
   102→      
   103→      # Combine results for each time point t across offsets
   104→      if (orig_nbasis == 1) {
   105→        hmat <- do.call(cbind, hmat_list) # Matrix with rows=time, cols=offsets
   106→        res <- if (summate) {
   107→          rowSums(hmat)
   108→        } else {
   109→          apply(hmat, 1, function(vals) vals[which.max(vals)])
   110→        }
   111→      } else {
   112→         # For multi-basis, sum matrices if summate=TRUE (difficult to define max sensibly)
   113→         if (!summate) {
   114→           warning("'summate = FALSE' is not fully supported for multi-basis HRFs in block_hrf; returning sum.")
   115→         }
   116→         res <- Reduce("+", hmat_list)
   117→      }
   118→    }
   119→    
   120→    # Apply normalization if requested
   121→    if (normalize) {
   122→      if (orig_nbasis == 1) {
   123→        peak_val <- max(abs(res), na.rm = TRUE)
   124→        if (!is.na(peak_val) && peak_val != 0) {
   125→          res <- res / peak_val
   126→        }
   127→      } else {
   128→        # Normalize each basis column independently
   129→        res <- apply(res, 2, function(basis_col) {
   130→           peak_val <- max(abs(basis_col), na.rm = TRUE)
   131→           if (!is.na(peak_val) && peak_val != 0) {
   132→             basis_col / peak_val
   133→           } else {
   134→             basis_col
   135→           }
   136→        })
   137→      }
   138→    }
   139→    return(res)
   140→  }
   141→
   142→  # Store parameters used for blocking
   143→  block_params <- list(
   144→      .width = width,
   145→      .precision = precision,
   146→      .half_life = half_life,
   147→      .summate = summate,
   148→      .normalize = normalize
   149→  )
   150→  
   151→  # Create new HRF object using as_hrf
   152→  as_hrf(
   153→    f = blocked_func,
   154→    name = paste0(orig_name, "_block(w=", width, ")"),
   155→    nbasis = orig_nbasis,
   156→    span = orig_span + width, # Span increases by the block width
   157→    params = c(orig_params, block_params) # Add block params for bookkeeping
   158→  )
   159→}
   160→
   161→
   162→#' Normalise an HRF Object
   163→#'
   164→#' Creates a new HRF object whose output is scaled such that the maximum absolute
   165→#' value of the response is 1.
   166→#'
   167→#' @param hrf The HRF object (of class `HRF`) to normalise.
   168→#'
   169→#' @return A new HRF object representing the normalised function.
   170→#' @details For multi-basis HRFs, each basis function (column) is normalised independently.
   171→#'
   172→#' @family HRF_decorator_functions
   173→#' @export
   174→#' @examples
   175→#' # Create a gaussian HRF with a peak value != 1
   176→#' gauss_unnorm <- as_hrf(function(t) 5 * dnorm(t, 6, 2), name="unnorm_gauss")
   177→#' # Normalise it
   178→#' gauss_norm <- normalise_hrf(gauss_unnorm)
   179→#' t_vals <- seq(0, 20, by = 0.1)
   180→#' max(gauss_unnorm(t_vals)) # Peak is > 1
   181→#' max(gauss_norm(t_vals))   # Peak is 1
   182→normalise_hrf <- function(hrf) {
   183→  assertthat::assert_that(inherits(hrf, "HRF"), msg = "Input 'hrf' must be an HRF object.")
   184→
   185→  # Original attributes
   186→  orig_name <- attr(hrf, "name")
   187→  orig_span <- attr(hrf, "span")
   188→  orig_nbasis <- nbasis(hrf)
   189→  orig_params <- attr(hrf, "params")
   190→
   191→  # Create the normalised function
   192→  normalised_func <- function(t) {
   193→    res <- hrf(t)
   194→    if (orig_nbasis == 1) {
   195→      peak_val <- max(abs(res), na.rm = TRUE)
   196→      if (!is.na(peak_val) && peak_val != 0) {
   197→        res <- res / peak_val
   198→      }
   199→    } else if (is.matrix(res)) {
   200→      # Normalise each basis column independently while preserving matrix shape
   201→      peaks <- apply(res, 2, function(basis_col) {
   202→        max(abs(basis_col), na.rm = TRUE)
   203→      })
   204→      peaks_safe <- ifelse(is.na(peaks) | peaks == 0, 1, peaks)
   205→      res <- sweep(res, 2, peaks_safe, "/")
   206→    }
   207→    # If it's not numeric or matrix (e.g., NULL or error result), return as is
   208→    return(res)
   209→  }
   210→
   211→  # Create new HRF object using as_hrf
   212→  as_hrf(
   213→    f = normalised_func,
   214→    name = paste0(orig_name, "_norm"),
   215→    nbasis = orig_nbasis,
   216→    span = orig_span,
   217→    params = c(orig_params, list(.normalised = TRUE)) # Add flag for bookkeeping
   218→  )
   219→}

#' Lag an HRF Object
#'
#' Creates a new HRF object by applying a temporal lag to an existing HRF object.
#'
#' @param hrf The HRF object (of class `HRF`) to lag.
#' @param lag The time lag in seconds to apply. Positive values shift the response later in time.
#'
#' @return A new HRF object representing the lagged function.
#'
#' @family HRF_decorator_functions
#' @export
#' @examples
#' lagged_spmg1 <- lag_hrf(HRF_SPMG1, 5)
#' # Evaluate at time 10; equivalent to HRF_SPMG1(10 - 5)
#' lagged_spmg1(10)
#' HRF_SPMG1(5)
lag_hrf <- function(hrf, lag) {
  assertthat::assert_that(inherits(hrf, "HRF"), msg = "Input 'hrf' must be an HRF object.")
  assertthat::assert_that(
    is.numeric(lag) && length(lag) == 1 && is.finite(lag),
    msg = "'lag' must be a single finite numeric value."
  )

  # Original attributes
  orig_name <- attr(hrf, "name")
  orig_span <- attr(hrf, "span")
  orig_nbasis <- nbasis(hrf)
  orig_params <- attr(hrf, "params")

  # Create the lagged function
  lagged_func <- function(t) {
    hrf(t - lag)
  }

  # Create new HRF object using as_hrf
  as_hrf(
    f = lagged_func,
    name = paste0(orig_name, "_lag(", lag, ")"),
    nbasis = orig_nbasis,
    span = orig_span + max(0, lag), # Increase span if lag is positive
    params = c(orig_params, list(.lag = lag)) # Add lag to params for bookkeeping
  )
}


#' Create a Blocked HRF Object
#'
#' Creates a new HRF object representing a response to a sustained (blocked)
#' stimulus by convolving the input HRF with a boxcar function of a given width.
#'
#' @param hrf The HRF object (of class `HRF`) to block.
#' @param width The width of the block in seconds.
#' @param precision The sampling precision in seconds used for the internal convolution (default: 0.1).
#' @param half_life The half-life of an optional exponential decay applied during the block (default: Inf, meaning no decay).
#' @param summate Logical; if TRUE (default), the responses from each time point within the block are summed. If FALSE, the maximum response at each time point is taken.
#' @param normalize Logical; if TRUE, the resulting blocked HRF is scaled so that its peak value is 1 (default: FALSE).
#'
#' @return A new HRF object representing the blocked function.
#'
#' @family HRF_decorator_functions
#' @export
#' @examples
#' blocked_spmg1 <- block_hrf(HRF_SPMG1, width = 5)
#' t_vals <- seq(0, 30, by = 0.5)
#' plot(t_vals, HRF_SPMG1(t_vals), type = 'l', col = "blue", ylab = "Response", xlab = "Time")
#' lines(t_vals, blocked_spmg1(t_vals), col = "red")
#' legend("topright", legend = c("Original", "Blocked (width=5)"), col = c("blue", "red"), lty = 1)
block_hrf <- function(hrf, width, precision = 0.1, half_life = Inf, summate = TRUE, normalize = FALSE) {
  assertthat::assert_that(inherits(hrf, "HRF"), msg = "Input 'hrf' must be an HRF object.")
  assertthat::assert_that(
    is.numeric(width) && length(width) == 1 && width >= 0 && is.finite(width),
    msg = "'width' must be a single non-negative finite numeric value."
  )
  assertthat::assert_that(
    is.numeric(precision) && length(precision) == 1 && precision > 0 && is.finite(precision),
    msg = "'precision' must be a single finite positive numeric value."
  )
  assertthat::assert_that(
    is.numeric(half_life) && length(half_life) == 1 && half_life > 0,
    msg = "'half_life' must be a single positive numeric value."
  )
  assertthat::assert_that(is.logical(summate) && length(summate) == 1, msg = "'summate' must be a single logical value.")
  assertthat::assert_that(is.logical(normalize) && length(normalize) == 1, msg = "'normalize' must be a single logical value.")

  # Original attributes
  orig_name <- attr(hrf, "name")
  orig_span <- attr(hrf, "span")
  orig_nbasis <- nbasis(hrf)
  orig_params <- attr(hrf, "params")

  # Create the blocked function
  blocked_func <- function(t) {
    if (width < precision) {
      # If width is negligible, just return the original hrf value
      res <- hrf(t)
    } else {
      samples <- seq(0, width, by = precision)
      hmat_list <- lapply(samples, function(offset) {
        decay_factor <- if (is.infinite(half_life)) 1 else exp(-log(2) * offset / half_life)
        hrf(t - offset) * decay_factor
      })
      
      # Combine results for each time point t across offsets
      if (orig_nbasis == 1) {
        hmat <- do.call(cbind, hmat_list) # Matrix with rows=time, cols=offsets
        res <- if (summate) {
          rowSums(hmat)
        } else {
          apply(hmat, 1, function(vals) vals[which.max(vals)])
        }
      } else {
         # For multi-basis, sum matrices if summate=TRUE (difficult to define max sensibly)
         if (!summate) {
           warning("'summate = FALSE' is not fully supported for multi-basis HRFs in block_hrf; returning sum.")
         }
         res <- Reduce("+", hmat_list)
      }
    }
    
    # Apply normalization if requested
    if (normalize) {
      if (orig_nbasis == 1) {
        peak_val <- max(abs(res), na.rm = TRUE)
        if (!is.na(peak_val) && peak_val != 0) {
          res <- res / peak_val
        }
      } else {
        # Normalize each basis column independently
        res <- apply(res, 2, function(basis_col) {
           peak_val <- max(abs(basis_col), na.rm = TRUE)
           if (!is.na(peak_val) && peak_val != 0) {
             basis_col / peak_val
           } else {
             basis_col
           }
        })
      }
    }
    return(res)
  }

  # Store parameters used for blocking
  block_params <- list(
      .width = width,
      .precision = precision,
      .half_life = half_life,
      .summate = summate,
      .normalize = normalize
  )
  
  # Create new HRF object using as_hrf
  as_hrf(
    f = blocked_func,
    name = paste0(orig_name, "_block(w=", width, ")"),
    nbasis = orig_nbasis,
    span = orig_span + width, # Span increases by the block width
    params = c(orig_params, block_params) # Add block params for bookkeeping
  )
}


#' Normalise an HRF Object
#'
#' Creates a new HRF object whose output is scaled such that the maximum absolute
#' value of the response is 1.
#'
#' @param hrf The HRF object (of class `HRF`) to normalise.
#'
#' @return A new HRF object representing the normalised function.
#' @details For multi-basis HRFs, each basis function (column) is normalised independently.
#'
#' @family HRF_decorator_functions
#' @export
#' @examples
#' # Create a gaussian HRF with a peak value != 1
#' gauss_unnorm <- as_hrf(function(t) 5 * dnorm(t, 6, 2), name="unnorm_gauss")
#' # Normalise it
#' gauss_norm <- normalise_hrf(gauss_unnorm)
#' t_vals <- seq(0, 20, by = 0.1)
#' max(gauss_unnorm(t_vals)) # Peak is > 1
#' max(gauss_norm(t_vals))   # Peak is 1
normalise_hrf <- function(hrf) {
  assertthat::assert_that(inherits(hrf, "HRF"), msg = "Input 'hrf' must be an HRF object.")

  # Original attributes
  orig_name <- attr(hrf, "name")
  orig_span <- attr(hrf, "span")
  orig_nbasis <- nbasis(hrf)
  orig_params <- attr(hrf, "params")

  # Create the normalised function
  normalised_func <- function(t) {
    res <- hrf(t)
    if (orig_nbasis == 1) {
      peak_val <- max(abs(res), na.rm = TRUE)
      if (!is.na(peak_val) && peak_val != 0) {
        res <- res / peak_val
      }
    } else if (is.matrix(res)) {
      # Normalise each basis column independently while preserving matrix shape
      peaks <- apply(res, 2, function(basis_col) {
        max(abs(basis_col), na.rm = TRUE)
      })
      peaks_safe <- ifelse(is.na(peaks) | peaks == 0, 1, peaks)
      res <- sweep(res, 2, peaks_safe, "/")
    }
    # If it's not numeric or matrix (e.g., NULL or error result), return as is
    return(res)
  }

  # Create new HRF object using as_hrf
  as_hrf(
    f = normalised_func,
    name = paste0(orig_name, "_norm"),
    nbasis = orig_nbasis,
    span = orig_span,
    params = c(orig_params, list(.normalised = TRUE)) # Add flag for bookkeeping
  )
}

#' @importFrom splines bs

NULL

#' HRF (hemodynamic response function) as a linear function of time
#'
#' The `hrf_time` function computes the value of an HRF, which is a simple linear function of time `t`, when `t` is greater than 0 and less than `maxt`.
#'
#' @param t A numeric value representing time in seconds.
#' @param maxt A numeric value representing the maximum time point in the domain. Default value is 22.
#' @return A numeric value representing the value of the HRF at the given time `t`.
#' @family hrf_functions
#' @export
#' @examples
#' # Compute the HRF value for t = 5 seconds with the default maximum time
#' hrf_val <- hrf_time(5)
#'
#' # Compute the HRF value for t = 5 seconds with a custom maximum time of 30 seconds
#' hrf_val_custom_maxt <- hrf_time(5, maxt = 30)
hrf_time <- function(t, maxt=22) {
  ifelse(t > 0 & t < maxt, t, 0)
}

# hrf_ident

hrf_ident <- function(t) {
  ifelse( t == 0, 1, 0)

#' # Compute the HRF values using the SPM canonical double gamma parameterization
#' hrf_values <- hrf_spmg1(time_points)
#' # Plot the HRF values
#' plot(time_points, hrf_values, type='l', main='SPM Canonical Double Gamma HRF')
hrf_spmg1 <- function(t, P1=5, P2=15,A1=.0833) {
 	ifelse(t < 0, 0, exp(-t)*(A1*t^P1 - 1.274527e-13*t^P2))
	
}


# Fast analytic first derivative for hrf_spmg1
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
hrf_spmg1_deriv <- function(t, P1 = 5, P2 = 15, A1 = .0833) {
  C <- 1.274527e-13
  ret <- numeric(length(t))
  pos <- t >= 0
  if (any(pos)) {
    t_pos <- t[pos]
    ret[pos] <- exp(-t_pos) * (A1 * t_pos^(P1 - 1) * (P1 - t_pos) -
                                 C   * t_pos^(P2 - 1) * (P2 - t_pos))
  }
  ret
}

[1] "Peak HRF value: 1.75394562063298 at time: 5"

content.text:
Perfect! So `0.4556` corresponds to the HRF value at time 9.6. If we have a lag of 4, then at time 9.6, the lagged HRF should give us the original HRF value at time 9.6 - 4 = 5.6. But the test is looking at search_times of 8,9,10,11,12, and expecting the maximum to be 0.4556.

Let me check if the test expectation is wrong. The issue is that with a lag of 4, the peak should shift from time 5 to time 9, and the search_times don't include time 9. Let me verify this:

input.command:
R --no-save --quiet -e "
library(fmrihrf)
hrf_spmg1_obj <- as_hrf(hrf_spmg1, name = 'spmg1', nbasis = 1, span = 30)

# Find the peak time of the original HRF
t_vals <- seq(0, 30, by = 0.01)
hrf_vals <- evaluate(hrf_spmg1_obj, t_vals)
peak_idx <- which.max(hrf_vals)
peak_time <- t_vals[peak_idx]
peak_value <- hrf_vals[peak_idx]
print(paste('Original HRF peak:', peak_value, 'at time:', peak_time))

# With a lag of 4, the peak should be at time 5 + 4 = 9
lagged_peak_time <- peak_time + 4
print(paste('Lagged HRF peak should be at time:', lagged_peak_time))

# Check the lagged HRF at the expected peak time
lagged_hrf <- lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 4.0)
lagged_peak_value <- evaluate(lagged_hrf, lagged_peak_time)
print(paste('Lagged HRF value at time', lagged_peak_time, ':', lagged_peak_value))

# Now check the search_times
search_times <- c(8.0, 9.0, 10.0, 11.0, 12.0)
print('Search times include 9.0, which is close to the expected peak at 9.0')
search_values <- evaluate(lagged_hrf, search_times)
print('Lagged HRF values at search_times:')
print(search_values)
print('Max at search_times:')
print(max(search_values))
"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<golden_test xmlns="http://golden-tests.org/schema">
  <metadata>
    <id>lag_hrf_decorator</id>
    <version>1.0</version>
    <description>Temporal lag decorator for HRF functions</description>
    <tags>
      <tag>core</tag>
      <tag>hrf</tag>
      <tag>decorator</tag>
      <tag>lag</tag>
      <tag>time_shift</tag>
    </tags>
  </metadata>
  
  <semantic_description>
    <purpose>Validate the lag_hrf function that applies temporal lag to existing HRF objects</purpose>
    <algorithm>
      The lag_hrf function is a decorator that applies a temporal lag to an existing HRF object.
      This creates a time-shifted version of the hemodynamic response, which is useful for 
      modeling delayed responses or temporal variations in neural processing.
      
      Mathematical behavior:
      - Time shifting: f_lagged(t) = f_original(t - lag)
      - Positive lag shifts response later in time (delayed response)
      - Negative lag shifts response earlier in time (anticipated response)
      - Preserves shape: only temporal position changes
      - Span adjustment: span_new = span_original + max(0, lag)
    </algorithm>
    <mathematical_properties>
      Key properties to validate:
      1. Time shift accuracy: exact temporal displacement
      2. Shape preservation: response magnitude unchanged at corresponding times
      3. Span adjustment: proper domain extension for positive lags
      4. Attribute inheritance: nbasis, name modification, parameter tracking
      5. Edge case handling: zero lag, negative lag, large lag values
      6. Mathematical equivalence: lag_hrf(h, L)(t) ≡ h(t - L)
    </mathematical_properties>
    <edge_cases>
      - Zero lag: should produce identical output to original
      - Negative lag: shifts response earlier, span unchanged
      - Large lag: significant span increase, delayed response
      - Multi-basis HRFs: preserve basis count and matrix structure
    </edge_cases>
  </semantic_description>
  
  <inputs>
    <input name="eval_times" type="numeric_vector">
      <description>Time points for evaluation</description>
      <value>c(0.0, 2.0, 5.0, 8.0, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0)</value>
    </input>
    <input name="comparison_times" type="numeric_vector">
      <description>Time points for equivalence checking</description>
      <value>c(-3.0, -1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 7.0, 12.0, 17.0)</value>
    </input>
    <input name="search_times" type="numeric_vector">
      <description>Time points for peak timing validation</description>
      <value>c(8.0, 9.0, 10.0, 11.0, 12.0)</value>
    </input>
  </inputs>
  
  <expected_outputs>
    <numeric_checks>
      <!-- Positive lag shift validation -->
      <check>
        <type>approximate</type>
        <location>evaluate(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0), eval_times)</location>
        <expected>[0.0, 0.0, 0.3716, 0.1253, 0.0498, 0.0045, 0.0005]</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-12</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Span adjustment for positive lag -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0), "span")</location>
        <expected>33.0</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-10</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Basis preservation -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>nbasis(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0))</location>
        <expected>1</expected>
        <tolerance>0</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Name modification -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0), "name")</location>
        <expected>spmg1_lag(3)</expected>
        <tolerance>0</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Negative lag: span unchanged -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_gaussian_obj, -2.0), "span")</location>
        <expected>30.0</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-10</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Zero lag identity -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_gamma_obj, 0.0), "span")</location>
        <expected>30.0</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-10</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Large lag boundary -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 25.0), "span")</location>
        <expected>55.0</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-10</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Peak timing shift validation -->
      <check>
        <type>approximate</type>
        <location>max(evaluate(lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 4.0), search_times))</location>
        <expected>0.4556</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-12</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Multi-basis preservation -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>nbasis(lag_hrf(hrf_bspline_obj, 2.5))</location>
        <expected>4</expected>
        <tolerance>0</tolerance>
      </check>
      
      <!-- Multi-basis span adjustment -->
      <check>
        <type>exact_value</type>
        <location>attr(lag_hrf(hrf_bspline_obj, 2.5), "span")</location>
        <expected>26.5</expected>
        <tolerance>1e-10</tolerance>
      </check>
    </numeric_checks>
  </expected_outputs>
  
  <implementations>
    <R><![CDATA[
      # Create base HRFs for testing
      hrf_spmg1_obj <- as_hrf(hrf_spmg1, name = "spmg1", nbasis = 1, span = 30)
      hrf_gaussian_obj <- as_hrf(function(t) hrf_gaussian(t, mean = 6, sd = 2), name = "gaussian", nbasis = 1, span = 30)
      hrf_gamma_obj <- as_hrf(function(t) hrf_gamma(t, shape = 6, rate = 1), name = "gamma", nbasis = 1, span = 30)
      hrf_bspline_obj <- as_hrf(function(t) hrf_bspline(t, N = 4), name = "bspline", nbasis = 4, span = 24)
      
      # Input vectors
      eval_times <- c(0.0, 2.0, 5.0, 8.0, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0)
      comparison_times <- c(-3.0, -1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 7.0, 12.0, 17.0)
      search_times <- c(8.0, 9.0, 10.0, 11.0, 12.0)
      
      # Test positive lag shift
      lagged_spmg1 <- lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0)
      lagged_values <- evaluate(lagged_spmg1, eval_times)
      equivalence_check <- evaluate(hrf_spmg1_obj, comparison_times)
      span_adjustment <- attr(lagged_spmg1, "span")
      nbasis_preserved <- nbasis(lagged_spmg1)
      name_modification <- attr(lagged_spmg1, "name")
      
      # Test negative lag shift
      lagged_gaussian_neg <- lag_hrf(hrf_gaussian_obj, -2.0)
      negative_lag_values <- evaluate(lagged_gaussian_neg, eval_times)
      span_no_increase <- attr(lagged_gaussian_neg, "span")
      name_negative_lag <- attr(lagged_gaussian_neg, "name")
      
      # Test zero lag identity
      lagged_gamma_zero <- lag_hrf(hrf_gamma_obj, 0.0)
      identity_values <- evaluate(lagged_gamma_zero, eval_times)
      span_unchanged <- attr(lagged_gamma_zero, "span")
      name_zero_lag <- attr(lagged_gamma_zero, "name")
      
      # Test large lag boundary
      lagged_spmg1_large <- lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 25.0)
      large_lag_values <- evaluate(lagged_spmg1_large, eval_times)
      large_span_increase <- attr(lagged_spmg1_large, "span")
      
      # Test peak timing shift
      lagged_spmg1_peak <- lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 4.0)
      peak_values <- evaluate(lagged_spmg1_peak, search_times)
      peak_shifted_correctly <- which.max(peak_values) == which.max(evaluate(hrf_spmg1_obj, search_times - 4.0))
      peak_magnitude_preserved <- max(peak_values)
      
      # Test multi-basis preservation
      lagged_bspline <- lag_hrf(hrf_bspline_obj, 2.5)
      multi_basis_result <- evaluate(lagged_bspline, eval_times)
      multi_basis_shape <- dim(multi_basis_result)
      nbasis_multi_preserved <- nbasis(lagged_bspline)
      span_multi_adjusted <- attr(lagged_bspline, "span")
    ]]></R>
    
    <Python><![CDATA[
      # Python implementation using closure pattern
      # Create a function that returns a closure capturing the lag value.
      # Use functools.wraps or similar for metadata preservation.
      # Ensure span calculation handles max(0, lag) correctly.
      # Implementation pending - refer to R reference for mathematical behavior
    ]]></Python>
    
    <Rust><![CDATA[
      // Rust implementation using higher-order functions or traits
      // Consider struct wrapping original HRF with lag field, or 
      // function returning Box<dyn Fn> for dynamic dispatch.
      // Ensure proper ownership and lifetime management.
      // Implementation pending - refer to R reference for mathematical behavior
    ]]></Rust>
  </implementations>
  
  <propagation_status>
    <implementation lang="R" status="completed" date="2024-01-15"/>
    <implementation lang="Python" status="pending"/>
    <implementation lang="Rust" status="pending"/>
  </propagation_status>
</golden_test>

content.text:
The validation tool must be elsewhere. Let me create a truly turnkey `/run-golden-tests` command that automatically copies the tools from the global location. Here's the corrected command:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# /run-golden-tests - Turnkey Golden Tests Runner
# Automatically copies tools from global location and runs tests

echo "🎯 GOLDEN TESTS EXECUTION - TURNKEY MODE"
echo "================================================"

# Global tools directory
GLOBAL_TOOLS_DIR="/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/translation/golden/tools"

# Check if this is a golden tests directory
if [ -d "golden_tests" ]; then
    cd golden_tests
    echo "✓ Found golden_tests directory"
elif [ -f "validate_golden_tests.py" ] || [ -d "specs" ]; then
    echo "✓ Already in golden tests directory"
else
    echo "❌ No golden_tests directory found"
    echo "This command works from:"
    echo "  1. Root of repository with golden_tests/ subdirectory"
    echo "  2. Inside the golden_tests/ directory itself"
    exit 1
fi

# Step 1: Copy essential tools from global location
echo ""
echo "🔄 STEP 1: SETTING UP TOOLS"
echo "================================================"

if [ -d "$GLOBAL_TOOLS_DIR" ]; then
    echo "Copying tools from global location..."
    
    # Copy R test runner
    if [ -f "$GLOBAL_TOOLS_DIR/golden_test_runner.R" ]; then
        cp "$GLOBAL_TOOLS_DIR/golden_test_runner.R" tools/
        echo "✓ Copied R test runner"
    fi
    
    # Copy dashboard generator
    if [ -f "$GLOBAL_TOOLS_DIR/enhanced_dashboard_generator.py" ]; then
        cp "$GLOBAL_TOOLS_DIR/enhanced_dashboard_generator.py" tools/
        echo "✓ Copied dashboard generator"
    fi
    
    # Copy validation tool if available
    if [ -f "$GLOBAL_TOOLS_DIR/validate_golden_tests.py" ]; then
        cp "$GLOBAL_TOOLS_DIR/validate_golden_tests.py" ./
        echo "✓ Copied validation tool"
    elif [ -f "/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/fmrihrf/golden_tests/tools/validate_golden_tests.py" ]; then
        cp "/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/fmrihrf/golden_tests/tools/validate_golden_tests.py" ./
        echo "✓ Copied validation tool from fmrihrf"
    fi
    
    echo "✅ Tools setup complete"
else
    echo "⚠️  Global tools directory not found at $GLOBAL_TOOLS_DIR"
    echo "Using existing local tools..."
fi

# Step 2: Schema Validation
echo ""
echo "🔒 STEP 2: SCHEMA VALIDATION"
echo "================================================"

if [ -f "validate_golden_tests.py" ]; then
    echo "Running schema validation..."
    python validate_golden_tests.py --project . --verbose
    SCHEMA_RESULT=$?
    
    if [ $SCHEMA_RESULT -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "✅ All XML files pass schema validation"
    else
        echo "❌ Schema validation failed"
        echo "Fix schema issues before running tests"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    echo "⚠️  Schema validator not found - skipping validation"
fi

# Step 3: Test Discovery
echo ""
echo "🔍 STEP 3: TEST DISCOVERY"
echo "================================================"

TOTAL_SPECS=$(find specs -name "*.xml" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$TOTAL_SPECS" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "❌ No test specifications found"
    exit 1
fi

echo "📋 Found $TOTAL_SPECS test specifications"

# Step 4: Run R Tests
echo ""
echo "🧪 STEP 4: RUNNING R TESTS"
echo "================================================"

TIMESTAMP=$(date +"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
RESULTS_FILE="golden_test_results_r_$TIMESTAMP.json"

if [ -f "tools/golden_test_runner.R" ]; then
    echo "Running R tests..."
    Rscript tools/golden_test_runner.R --output "$RESULTS_FILE" --verbose
    R_EXIT_CODE=$?
    
    if [ $R_EXIT_CODE -eq 0 ] && [ -f "$RESULTS_FILE" ]; then
        echo "✅ R tests completed successfully"
        
        # Show summary
        R_SUMMARY=$(Rscript -e "
library(jsonlite)
data <- fromJSON('$RESULTS_FILE')
s <- data\$summary
cat(sprintf('📊 R Results: %d/%d passed (%.1f%%), %d failed, %d errors\\n', 
    s\$passed_tests, s\$total_tests, s\$passed_tests/s\$total_tests*100, 
    s\$failed_tests, s\$error_tests))
" 2>/dev/null)
        echo "$R_SUMMARY"
    else
        echo "❌ R tests failed"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    echo "❌ R test runner not found"
    exit 1
fi

# Step 5: Generate Dashboard
echo ""
echo "📊 STEP 5: GENERATING DASHBOARD"
echo "================================================"

if [ -f "tools/enhanced_dashboard_generator.py" ]; then
    DASHBOARD_DIR="dashboard_$TIMESTAMP"
    
    echo "Generating interactive dashboard..."
    python tools/enhanced_dashboard_generator.py \
        --project . \
        --results "$RESULTS_FILE" \
        --output "$DASHBOARD_DIR"
    
    if [ -f "$DASHBOARD_DIR/index.html" ]; then
        echo "✅ Dashboard generated: $DASHBOARD_DIR/index.html"
    else
        echo "⚠️  Dashboard generation failed"
    fi
else
    echo "⚠️  Dashboard generator not found - skipping"
fi

# Step 6: Summary
echo ""
echo "================================================"
echo "✅ GOLDEN TESTS EXECUTION COMPLETE"
echo "================================================"
echo ""
echo "📊 Results: $RESULTS_FILE"
if [ -f "$DASHBOARD_DIR/index.html" ]; then
    echo "📈 Dashboard: $DASHBOARD_DIR/index.html"
fi
echo ""
echo "⚡ Quick Actions:"
echo "  View results: cat $RESULTS_FILE | jq ."
if [ -f "$DASHBOARD_DIR/index.html" ]; then
    echo "  Open dashboard: open $DASHBOARD_DIR/index.html"
fi
echo "  Re-run tests: /run-golden-tests"
echo ""
```

The key changes to make this turnkey:

1. **Automatic tool copying**: Copies essential tools from `~/code/translation/golden/tools/` to local `tools/` directory
2. **Fallback handling**: If global tools aren't found, uses existing local tools
3. **R-only focus**: Removed Python test execution since you specified R-only
4. **Self-contained**: Everything needed is copied locally, no external dependencies

For the current session, the tools are already in place, but for future use, this approach will ensure any golden tests directory can be run without manual setup.

     7→
     8→## Understanding Golden Tests
     9→

    11→- **Semantic focus**: WHAT code should do, not HOW
    12→- **Numeric validation**: Matrix dimensions, values, statistical properties  
    13→- **Language agnosticism**: Behavior descriptions, not implementation details

   138→
   139→## Step 4: Install Schema Prevention Measures
   140→

   177→jobs:

   199→

   687→## Step 8: Create Language-Specific Validator
   688→
   689→```bash
   690→# Create validator for the source language
   691→mkdir -p "golden_tests/validators/$PROJECT_LANG"
   692→
   693→case $PROJECT_LANG in
   694→    "Python")
   695→        cat > "golden_tests/validators/$PROJECT_LANG/validate_golden_tests.py" << 'EOF'
   696→#!/usr/bin/env python3
   697→"""
   698→Golden test validator for Python implementations.
   699→Based on the comprehensive golden tests methodology.
   700→
   701→This is a starter template. For a complete validator, copy from:
   702→~/code/fmrihrf/golden_tests/validators/R/validate_golden_tests.R
   703→and adapt for Python.
   704→"""
   705→
   706→import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
   707→import numpy as np
   708→from typing import Dict, Any, List, Union
   709→import sys
   710→import os
   711→
   712→class GoldenTestValidator:
   713→    def __init__(self, tolerance_override=None):
   714→        self.tolerance_override = tolerance_override
   715→        self.namespace = {'gt': 'http://golden-tests.org/schema'}
   716→        
   717→    def validate_golden_test(self, xml_path: str, implementation_result: Any, 
   718→                           tolerance_override: float = None) -> bool:
   719→        """
   720→        Validate a golden test against an implementation result.
   721→        
   722→        Args:
   723→            xml_path: Path to the golden test XML file
   724→            implementation_result: Result from the implementation to validate
   725→            tolerance_override: Override for all tolerance values (optional)
   726→            
   727→        Returns:
   728→            bool: Whether all checks passed
   729→        """
   730→        test_data = self.parse_golden_test(xml_path)
   731→        
   732→        # Run all numeric checks
   733→        checks = test_data.get('expected_outputs', {}).get('numeric_checks', [])
   734→        all_passed = True
   735→        
   736→        for check in checks:
   737→            result = self.perform_numeric_check(implementation_result, check, tolerance_override)
   738→            if not result:
   739→                print(f"FAILED: {check.get('type')} check at {check.get('location')}")
   740→                print(f"  Expected: {check.get('expected')} Tolerance: {check.get('tolerance')}")
   741→                all_passed = False
   742→            else:
   743→                print(f"PASSED: {check.get('type')} check at {check.get('location')}")
   744→        
   745→        if all_passed:
   746→            print("All golden test checks PASSED")
   747→        else:
   748→            print("Some golden test checks FAILED")
   749→        
   750→        return all_passed
   751→        
   752→    def parse_golden_test(self, xml_path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   753→        """Parse a golden test XML file."""
   754→        tree = ET.parse(xml_path)
   755→        root = tree.getroot()
   756→        
   757→        # Basic parsing - extend as needed
   758→        test_data = {
   759→            'metadata': self._extract_text_elements(root, ['id', 'version', 'description']),
   760→            'expected_outputs': self._extract_expected_outputs(root)
   761→        }
   762→        
   763→        return test_data
   764→    
   765→    def perform_numeric_check(self, data: Any, check: Dict[str, Any], 
   766→                            tolerance_override: float = None) -> bool:
   767→        """Perform a single numeric check."""
   768→        # TODO: Implement complete numeric checking logic
   769→        # This is a basic template - see GOLDEN_TESTS.md for full implementation guidance
   770→        
   771→        check_type = check.get('type')
   772→        expected = check.get('expected')
   773→        tolerance = tolerance_override or float(check.get('tolerance', 1e-10))
   774→        
   775→        # Basic example - extend for your specific data structures
   776→        if check_type == 'exact_value':
   777→            if check.get('location') == 'length(output)':
   778→                return len(data) == int(expected)
   779→        elif check_type == 'approximate':
   780→            if check.get('location') == 'sum(output)':
   781→                return abs(sum(data) - float(expected)) < tolerance
   782→                
   783→        # TODO: Implement range checks, statistical checks, etc.
   784→        return False
   785→    
   786→    def _extract_text_elements(self, root, elements):
   787→        """Extract text content from specified elements."""
   788→        result = {}
   789→        for elem in elements:
   790→            node = root.find(f'.//gt:{elem}', self.namespace)
   791→            if node is not None:
   792→                result[elem] = node.text
   793→        return result
   794→    
   795→    def _extract_expected_outputs(self, root):
   796→        """Extract expected outputs from XML."""
   797→        checks = []
   798→        check_nodes = root.findall('.//gt:check', self.namespace)
   799→        
   800→        for check_node in check_nodes:
   801→            check = {}
   802→            for field in ['type', 'location', 'expected', 'tolerance', 'min', 'max', 'property']:
   803→                elem = check_node.find(f'gt:{field}', self.namespace)
   804→                if elem is not None:
   805→                    check[field] = elem.text
   806→            checks.append(check)
   807→            
   808→        return {'numeric_checks': checks}
   809→
   810→# Helper function for null coalescing  
   811→def null_coalesce(x, y):
   812→    return y if x is None else x

   815→    print("Golden test validator template for Python")
   816→    print("Implement your specific validation logic following GOLDEN_TESTS.md")
   817→    print("Example usage:")
   818→    print("  validator = GoldenTestValidator()")
   819→    print("  result = your_implementation()")
   820→    print("  success = validator.validate_golden_test('test.xml', result)")

   826→    "R")
   827→        cat > "golden_tests/validators/$PROJECT_LANG/validate_golden_tests.R" << 'EOF'
   828→# Golden test validator for R implementations
   829→# Based on the comprehensive golden tests methodology
   830→#
   831→# This is a starter template. For a complete validator, copy from:
   832→# ~/code/fmrihrf/golden_tests/validators/R/validate_golden_tests.R
   833→
   834→library(xml2)
   835→
   836→#' Validate a golden test against an R implementation
   837→#'
   838→#' @param xml_path Path to the golden test XML file
   839→#' @param implementation_result Result from the R implementation to validate
   840→#' @param tolerance_override Override for all tolerance values (optional)
   841→#' @return Logical indicating whether all checks passed
   842→validate_golden_test <- function(xml_path, implementation_result, tolerance_override = NULL) {
   843→  # Parse the XML file
   844→  test_data <- parse_golden_test(xml_path)
   845→  
   846→  # Run all numeric checks
   847→  checks <- test_data$expected_outputs$numeric_checks
   848→  all_passed <- TRUE
   849→  
   850→  for (check in checks) {
   851→    result <- perform_numeric_check(implementation_result, check, tolerance_override)
   852→    if (!result) {
   853→      cat("FAILED:", check$type, "check at", check$location, "\n")
   854→      cat("  Expected:", check$expected, "Tolerance:", check$tolerance, "\n")
   855→      all_passed <- FALSE
   856→    } else {
   857→      cat("PASSED:", check$type, "check at", check$location, "\n")
   858→    }
   859→  }
   860→  
   861→  if (all_passed) {
   862→    cat("All golden test checks PASSED\n")
   863→  } else {
   864→    cat("Some golden test checks FAILED\n")
   865→  }
   866→  
   867→  return(all_passed)
   868→}
   869→
   870→#' Parse a golden test XML file
   871→#'
   872→#' @param xml_path Path to the XML file
   873→#' @return List containing parsed test data
   874→parse_golden_test <- function(xml_path) {
   875→  doc <- xml2::read_xml(xml_path)
   876→  
   877→  # Extract basic metadata
   878→  metadata <- list(
   879→    id = xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_first(doc, ".//id")),
   880→    version = xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_first(doc, ".//version")),
   881→    description = xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_first(doc, ".//description"))
   882→  )
   883→  
   884→  # Extract expected outputs and checks
   885→  check_nodes <- xml2::xml_find_all(doc, ".//numeric_checks/check")
   886→  checks <- lapply(check_nodes, function(node) {
   887→    check <- list(
   888→      type = xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_first(node, "type")),
   889→      location = xml2::xml_text(xml2::xml_find_first(node, "location"))
   890→    )
   891→    
   892→    # Optional fields
   893→    optional_fields <- c("expected", "tolerance", "min", "max", "property")
   894→    for (field in optional_fields) {
   895→      field_node <- xml2::xml_find_first(node, field)
   896→      if (!is.na(field_node)) {
   897→        if (field %in% c("expected", "tolerance", "min", "max")) {
   898→          check[[field]] <- as.numeric(xml2::xml_text(field_node))
   899→        } else {
   900→          check[[field]] <- xml2::xml_text(field_node)
   901→        }
   902→      }
   903→    }
   904→    
   905→    return(check)
   906→  })
   907→  
   908→  return(list(
   909→    metadata = metadata,
   910→    expected_outputs = list(numeric_checks = checks)
   911→  ))
   912→}
   913→
   914→#' Perform a single numeric check
   915→#'
   916→#' @param data The implementation result data
   917→#' @param check The check specification
   918→#' @param tolerance_override Override tolerance value
   919→#' @return Logical indicating whether the check passed
   920→perform_numeric_check <- function(data, check, tolerance_override = NULL) {
   921→  # TODO: Implement complete checking logic
   922→  # This is a basic template - see GOLDEN_TESTS.md for full implementation
   923→  
   924→  # Use override tolerance if provided
   925→  tolerance <- if (!is.null(tolerance_override)) {
   926→    tolerance_override
   927→  } else {
   928→    check$tolerance %||% 1e-10
   929→  }
   930→  
   931→  # Basic examples - extend for your specific data structures
   932→  if (check$type == "exact_value" && check$location == "length(output)") {
   933→    return(length(data) == check$expected)
   934→  } else if (check$type == "approximate" && check$location == "sum(output)") {
   935→    return(abs(sum(data) - check$expected) < tolerance)
   936→  }
   937→  
   938→  # TODO: Implement range checks, statistical checks, etc.
   939→  warning(paste("Check type not implemented:", check$type))
   940→  return(FALSE)
   941→}
   942→
   943→# Helper function for null coalescing
   944→`%||%` <- function(x, y) if (is.null(x)) y else x
   945→
   946→# Example usage:
   947→# result <- your_function(test_input)
   948→# validate_golden_test("specs/core/starter_template.xml", result)
   949→EOF

   951→        ;;

   955→        echo "  See GOLDEN_TESTS.md for guidance on validator implementation"
   956→        ;;
   957→esac
   958→```
